Marcello Parks is an AI-native music artist — a producer whose instruments are the prompt, the patch, the edit, and the ear. Told in college that music wasn't a path worth walking, he shelved the dream. Generative tools handed it back, louder and wider than before. Rooted in dance but refusing to be pinned to a single genre, Marcello treats every session as open frontier — ambient textures one week, raw percussive energy the next, then somewhere the charts haven't mapped yet. What reaches you isn't everything he makes. It's what survives the filter: a trained ear curating each release, because taste is the instrument no model can replace.

Fresh to the scene, Nistagmuss is a brand new house, techno, tech-house producer originally from the West Midlands, now based in the South. 

Drawing from the UK’s deep-rooted club culture, his sound blends groove-led rhythms, hypnotic percussion, and uplifting textures that sit between underground energy and feel-good dancefloor moments.

With a focus on movement and connection, Nistagmuss crafts tracks designed for DJs—whether it’s late-night sessions, open-air sets, or peak-time floors. Rooted in rhythm and driven by instinct, his music delivers a balance of raw energy and melodic warmth that keeps crowds locked in.

About ESCON:

ESCON! Verschrieben haben wir uns mit diesem Namen der wunderbaren elektronischen Tanzmusik. Unser Anspruch und unsere Leidenschaft haben uns dazu bewegt, die Stadt, in der wir leben, mit Events und musikalischem Input zu versorgen. Das Wichtigste an unserem Weltverstehen ist dabei, dass jeder Mensch, der Musik, Liebe, das Leben und vor allem ein friedliches Miteinander genauso liebt wie wir, von uns als UNSER FREUND bezeichnet wird! Enge Verbundenheit zu Kunst & Kultur hat uns dazu bewegt, die Locations der Events auf ihre ganz eigenen Arten und Weisen zu dekorieren. Das heißt, jede ESCON Location wird es so nur einmal geben. Werdet Teil davon und macht somit jede Feierei zu dem, was es ist: positive Erlebnisse und Erinnerungen. We all will never be forgotten. In diesem Sinne: feiert, rastet, tastet, fühlt, spürt, lebt und lasst leben! Helft uns dabei, das Aussterben der Subkultur in München zu verhindern!

LOUGHO, a 24 year-old DJ and producer hailing from Ireland, is making waves in the electronic music scene. LOUGHO is on a mission to see his music soar to new heights. With a relatively short time in the industry, his talent and passion have already led to significant accomplishments.

LOUGHO began his journey into music production in 2018, and in a short span, he has managed to sign multiple tracks to record labels. Notably, he secured “Right Now” to Viviana Casanova ' label, VASSNOVA, which is a testament to his rising profile within the industry.

His music style is diverse, ranging from dance tracks to heavy techno beats, showcasing his versatility as an artist. This willingness to explore various electronic music subgenres and his dedication to perfecting his craft have contributed to his growing recognition in the music community.

With a background in DJing that began in 2019, LOUGHO is not only honing his skills behind the decks but also developing his unique sound as a producer. As he continues to evolve his music and share his creativity with the world, his journey as an "up and coming" DJ and producer is one to watch closely. LOUGHO’s determination and talent hold the promise of an exciting and successful career in the electronic music industry.

Arne Jungberg is a DJ and electronic music enthusiast from Stade near Hamburg, Germany. His passion for sound developed early on, beginning with classical guitar lessons before discovering electronic music through electro-punk and synthesizers.
In 2013, he founded the Sternberk Club & Bar, where he gained his first experiences behind the decks and began hosting events and underground raves with local and regional artists.
As a member of Sinus Recordings, Arne plays regularly in techno clubs in and outside of Hamburg and has become an essential part of the label. Alongside his work as a DJ, he is involved in social media management and A&R. He also runs his own podcast, focusing on electronic music, artists, and scene culture.
His sound is hard to categorize, sitting perfectly between minimal and psytech — driving and straightforward, with a balanced mix of hypnotic and trippy elements.

A hitmaker and multi-platinum selling artist, D.O.D is the creative force behind some of dance music’s most recognisable contemporary tracks. From the soaring success of BRIT-nominated 'Somedays' with Sonny Fodera and Jazzy, which landed a #5 position on the UK Official Top 40, to the UK Top 20 hit 'So Much In Love,' and breakout hit 'Still Sleepless,' his music has earned him devoted fan bases across the globe. With over 500 million streams to his name and career-defining achievements such as a #1 spot on the Official Dance Singles Chart and multiple Beatport #1s, D.O.D’s sound resonates far and wide.
As a global touring artist, D.O.D boasts sold-out headline shows across the UK, Australia, and North America. From intimate club nights to renowned international festivals, he has graced some of dance music’s most iconic stages, including Tomorrowland, Ultra, Creamfields, and The Warehouse Project, securing him a place on the prestigious DJ Mag Top 100.
Having spent over a decade honing his signature style—a vibrant and instantly recognisable house sound deeply rooted in the UK—D.O.D has carved a space for himself in dance music’s elite.

https://www.djdod.com/

Montreal is the second largest metropolis in Canada, a multi cultural heaven brimming with talent. Its rich and vibrant heritage has yielded many high caliber artists.

Once such artist, Patrick Guay was born to a proud, working class French Canadian family. As is natural in Quebec, music and dance were part of life and Patrick excelled at both at a young age. Focused
and hardworking, at 19 he joined a professional dance troupe.

It was the early nineties-the heyday of the Montreal dance scene. Patrick was part of the tightly knit dance club community that witnessed infamous nights at K.O.X, Playground and later Stereo. The pre-911 circuit scene in Montreal was all about large-scale events, which attracted an international crowd. Over the top décor and shows worthy of Broadway were the norm. Award winning producer Jean-Pierre Pérusse noticed Patrick’s talents and appointed him as choreographer for the BBCM dancers- a task Patrick took to with enthusiasm and dedication. His innovative and exciting shows wowed thousands at the Olympic Stadium for the Black & Blue Party, and other events produced by the BBCM.

It was only natural for multi talented Patrick to segway in to DJ’ing in 2002. He was fueled by the desire to become independent and take complete artistic control over the music that accompanied his shows. It did not take long before he mastered the craft of mixing, but he took his time and entered the dance club scene cautiously, preferring to absolutely perfect his style before coming out officially as a DJ.

He got his first start at the legendary Church daylight parties in Montreal – the golden era of Montreal after hours clubbing. The Church parties, though dismantled in 2006 are still fresh in the minds of many Montrealers. Club kids mixed with drag queens and circuit boys, it was a big family, the place to be- and the place to be seen. He quickly started to gain recognition and a growing following and was next awarded a coveted monthly residency at Parking Nightclub and at Stereo Afterhours. In only a few short years he was consistently getting booked at Parking to open for Manny Lehman and Anna Paula due to his ability to bring the crowd up to the right level of excitement. In recent times he has opened for such greats as Junior Vasquez and Offer Nissim. He’s played repeated gigs in Montreal, Toronto, Vancouver and Provincetown, and is now aiming at spinning at parties in the Americas and Europe.

Patrick’s musical style is versatile; swiftly and seamlessly moving through house diva anthems to dark sexy beats- tribal and groovy with an edge. He is a multidisciplinary artist whose roots are forever cemented in the dance floor. Unique and attractive, he thrives on performing and focusing his energy on the crowd. With discipline and passion, he unleashes his creativity and produces an unforgettable atmosphere on the dance floor. By Kat Coric

His productions have earned recognition across the global techno community, with releases on respected labels such as Of Us Records, Deep Tales, Androgyne, and Lelantus Records. Drawing inspiration from both classic techno foundations and modern melodic influences, Tumzz crafts sets that balance driving rhythms with atmospheric storytelling.

This signature approach has led him to perform at leading venues including Kompass Club, Club Vaag, Sotto’s, and Charlatan, while also expanding internationally with appearances in Malmö, ADE, Void Berlin, Mendoza Malta, Ministry of Sound London, and Mazo Club Poland.

His music has received support from key artists in the scene such as Korolova, Mila Journée, Fideles, Argy, Armin van Buuren, David Guetta, Tiësto, Lost Frequencies, and Kevin de Vries, further solidifying his presence within the international circuit.

Driven by passion and a clear artistic vision, Tumzz continues to push boundaries—both in the studio and behind the decks.

Before the Spotlight: The Rise of The Creatures in 1960s Montreal.
In 1963, while Beatlemania gripped the UK and Motown was shaking up the charts, something electric was taking shape a world away—in a small Montreal apartment. Four teenagers, fueled by the raw energy of the British rock invasion and the soulful pull of American R&B, came together to form a band. With the support of our teenage manager—and his ever-patient mother who let us rehearse in their apartment—we found our footing, even if the neighbors weren’t thrilled about it.
We called ourselves The Creatures, inspired by the wave of iconic names that shaped our musical DNA: The Everley Brothers, The Beatles, The Stones, The Animals, Herman's Hermits, Dave Clark Five, Buddy Holly & The Crickets, The Ventures, The Surfaris, The Searchers, The Kinks, The Rascals, Paul Revere & The Raiders, The McCoys, The Righteous Brothers, Paul Butterfield Blues Band, James Brown, The Temptations, Marvin Gaye, Ray Charles, Joe Tex, Little Richard, and so many more. In a matter of months, we had three full sets of covers—both instrumentals and vocals—ready to roll.
It was an exhilarating time. While Petula Clark sang about going “Downtown,” we were playing four nights a week—two for rehearsals, two for gigs. Our sound was shaped by rock, funk, and R&B, and as we played together, we grew tighter, louder, and more ambitious.
Our first professional gig was at École Lajoie in Outremont, Quebec. We didn’t have the best gear, but we had something just as powerful—drive, rhythm, and chemistry. Soon, The Creatures started catching fire. With new equipment in hand and word spreading, we were playing packed shows at high schools, clubs, and major venues. One of the biggest was Dave Boxer’s legendary Bonaventure Curling Club, where 3,000 fans would turn up on a Saturday night. Boxer, a well-known DJ on CFCF Radio, was instrumental in giving Canadian bands their shot—including us.
But the peak came when we played the Montreal Forum the same stage that once welcomed The Beatles—at Dave Boxer’s "Bring Your Lunch Hopsville," alongside ten of Canada’s top acts. The show ran from 1pm to 10pm, and the Forum was filled wall-to-wall with screaming teenagers. For us, it was unforgettable.
The Montreal music scene in the ’60s was alive with sound and style—from Carnaby Street fashions to the explosion of long hair, bell-bottoms, and platform shoes. Local legends like, The JayBees, The Haunted, Les Classels, Trixie & The Inmates, Bartholomew Plus Three, The Rabble, The Mersey Makers, We Five, and All of Us, helped define an era. And in that mix stood The Creatures—young, hungry, and chasing the beat.
Though the band eventually split in the mid-60s for personal reasons, the spirit of The Creatures never faded. Today, that spirit lives on through the original music of Morr Serber, whose songwriting carries the echoes of a time when four teens from Montreal believed they could change the world—with guitars, harmonies, and a dream.

I pour my deepest emotions into every song I create. Each one reflects how I feel, what I’ve been through, and the stories that touch my heart.
I create heartfelt songs about love, heartbreak, healing, and the most personal moments in life.
My dream is to be heard, felt, and understood one song at a time.

Additionally, I am an AI music creator, and I truly appreciate this tool for giving me the chance to be heard and to create the beautiful songs I’ve always dreamed of since I was a child. Growing up, I never had the resources or support to develop my talent.

Now, through AI music, I finally have the opportunity to bring my dreams to life. I’m not trying to become a famous artist, I understand that AI music can’t replace the true talent of a person. But for me, it’s about experiencing the passion that was once neglected and expressing my emotions through song.

It honestly feels amazing, and sometimes I wish it were all real. Still, I’m deeply grateful for this chance.. Thank you, AI, for helping me find my voice.

Auteur-compositeur de chansons françaises qui n'attendent que de bons interprètes. 
Après des études au conservatoire et musicologie à la Sorbonne, j'ai enseigné l'éducation musicale en école élémentaire de 1982 à 2021 à Paris.

Dès 1989, alors que je découvrais la MAO, j'ai commencé à faire des centaines d'arrangements de chansons pour enfants ou adultes dans tous les styles. Cette solide expérience m'a amené dans un premier temps à composer des chansons pour mes élèves. Puis, je me suis lancé dans un répertoire pour adultes. 139 chansons à ce jour !

Mon répertoire va de la danse traditionnelle française (La bourrée du maumarié), à l’EDM (La tête dans les étoiles) en passant par la musique classique (Double concerto pour violon, violoncelle et orchestre à cordes) et la chanson française, le gros du panier, avec le plus souvent cette pointe d’humour qui fait tant défaut aujourd’hui dans beaucoup de chansons, (ce qui est étonnant quand on voit autant d’humoristes fleurir aux six points de l’Hexagone…)

Même si certains apprécient ma façon de chanter, mes chansons sont faites pour de vrais interprètes qui aiment monter sur scène.

Né en 1961 à Paris.
Membre SACEM

Author-composer and performer... by default of French songs. 
After studying at the Conservatoire and musicology at the Sorbonne, I taught music education to elementary school pupils from 1982 to 2021.

As early as 1989, when I was just discovering computer-assisted music, I started making hundreds of arrangements of songs for children and adults in all styles, to get them to sing along. This solid experience initially led me to compose songs for my students. Then I started working on a repertoire for adults. 139 songs to date!

My repertoire ranges from traditional French dance (La bourrée du maumarié), to EDM (La tête dans les étoiles), classical music (Double concerto for violin, cello and string orchestra) and French chanson, the mainstay of my repertoire, most often with that touch of humor so lacking in so many songs these days (which is astonishing when you see so many humorists flourishing at the six points of the Hexagon...).

Even if some people like the way I sing, my songs are made for real performers who like to get up on stage.

Noel is a writer of songs and stories rather than a musical performer. His songs have been described as reflective as they are motivated by the struggles, celebrations and tensions of everyday life. His musical stories typically address issues as diverse as cancer and homelessness, addictions, love, loss and troubled relationships. These songs are led by the lyrics and are a mix of folk, country and blues.
Inspired by a diverse range of music from Leonard Cohen to Jason Isbell, Noel's songs took years to find their voice.Noel was born in Ireland and currently lives there but spent many years living and working in Africa. Much of his inspiration has been drawn from his own life experiences including his more recent journey with cancer. ‘Stars in Heaven’ is one of a number of songs dealing with cancer.  This particular song was chosen by Music Beats Cancer after submissions by over 800 other artists.
Noel says “ I really hope that the songs can reach a much bigger audience. I also hope that the songs can help, even in some small way, in connecting with people and in raising awareness and support for the issues they address."

Songwriting has been a lifelong passion for me, dating back to elementary school when I first began crafting stories, poems, and eventually lyrics. As a former drummer in various bands, I always relied on others to bring my words to life musically. However, after a long hiatus from drumming, I decided to take the leap and learn to compose music to accompany my lyrics. Through classes and dedication, I've embarked on a new journey, breathing fresh life into old lyrics and penning new ones. You'll find a mix of older and newer works here. Especially for some of the lyrics. 

Music has been a part of me for as long as I can remember. One of my earliest memories is being completely entranced by 'The Little Drummer Boy' as a very young child. There was something about the rhythm and the melody that just captivated me. I must have played it a thousand times, wearing the record out, and from that moment on, I was hooked. I knew I wanted to be a drummer and to create that magic for others. 

But I was too young then, and of course, I couldn't afford a drum set just yet. So, I turned to writing as a way to express myself. As I mentioned above, I started with stories, then poems, and eventually, lyrics. I found that writing was not only a way to tap into my creativity but also a way to process my emotions and experiences. 

As I grew older, my love for music only deepened. I realized that music has this incredible power to transport us to different times and places, to evoke emotions and memories that we thought were long forgotten. It's a universal language that can bring people together like nothing else can. 

For me, writing lyrics is a cathartic experience. It's a way for me to tap into my emotions, to process the world around me, and to create something that I hope will resonate with others. And when I hear my words come to life in a song, it's truly magical. It's a feeling that never gets old, and it's what keeps me coming back to music time and time again. 

I love many genres of music and artists of many genres. And if you listen to some of my songs, you will find that I like to make songs of different genres as well as mix different genres into each song. One of my all-time favorite bands is Evanescence, and I have made a lot of songs where I have tried my best to emulate that sound. 

I hope you enjoy some of my songs. And thanks so much to everyone who has been listening so far. I truly appreciate it. 🙏🫶❤️

Brief Story of my musical life!
Hi, dear music lovers, 
*My name is Johan F. Langhendries and at the age of two, I became interested in music. Believe it or not, I was a very quiet child and I often listened to music on my father's radio. Jazz, Rock, Classic, just everything! A little later, at age 4 and 5, my grandmother learned me what a C-chord was on her piano, in the different octaves. Later on, she learned me also the other chord. At the age of six, I started making my own drums with wash-powder boxes and I used wooden kitchen spoons to make the sound of a “drum”. When I was 8, several albums like for example; “Let it be” by The Beatles, Block Buster by The Sweet, Black & Blue by The Rolling Stones, etc... were a part of my drum exercise experiences as a young kid. 

° At the age 10, I learned to play on a real drum-kit and trumpet and was also following music theory in two different music schools, for six years, to fill up my evenings on Tuesdays and Thursdays. On Saturday morning and Wednesday afternoon, I had to go to our music theory class and we learned how to write music, etc… At the age of 12, I had my first second-hand drum-kit and at sixteen i started playing in a Punk-Rockband. Lots of fun indeed! At age 18 I  went to study at private school ‘Haleweyn ‘Jazz-Studio’ in Antwerp and learned a lot more about Jazz music and how to play it... I Love to play Jazz, but Funk and Rock became the biggest part of my drumming experience.

– EXPERIENCE; I’ve played as a professional drummer in many bands (Belgium) like ‘Citizen Jane’ (Jazz/Pop) for 5 years with one CD recording and many concerts. I’ve been a studio recording drummer and have worked 40 years as a drummer;  Live concerts, Recording drummer and albums with different rock bands, etc. I still do and learn of course!

– MY PROJECTS SINCE 2004 are; Wannislas(aka) and Abstinent Dazzle (Band). With these two projects, I make some Funky, Dance, Reggae, Rock & Jazz (Fusion) music.    In my third project; Johan Franz Lang (feat. Space Orchestra) I make a kind of contemporary chill-out, easy listening music. 

- I followed a sound engineering class for 2 years (age 29) and do the mastering for my albums and sometimes for other artists - 

° Now, I hope you will enjoy my music and I like to get comments and positive critics if there are some, and of course I appreciate too see some sharing of my music on social media!

“Deep, atmospheric, funky and hypnotic.”

That’s how ZDEPTH describes his sound.

Based in Sofia, ZDEPTH (Dimitar Kirov) is a DJ and producer known for crafting hypnotic house grooves layered with deep atmospheres, brooding basslines, refined synth work, and subtle vocal hooks. Drawing inspiration from years spent raving across Europe’s major clubs and festivals, his sound moves seamlessly between House, Minimal/Deep Tech, Progressive House, and Techno.

Active since 2019, ZDEPTH quickly built a strong presence on the Bulgarian club circuit, performing at respected venues such as Yalta Club, Mixtape 5, Club CODE, and Makalali Beach Bar. Along the way, he has shared the stage with internationally recognized artists including Metodi Hristov, Sasha Carassi, Cosmic Boys, Pavel Petrov, Balthazar & Jackrock, Paride Saraceni, and Audiojack.

Beyond the DJ booth, ZDEPTH curates his own event series Infinite Grooves, dedicated to the modern club sound and bringing together forward-thinking house and techno selectors.

In 2025, ZDEPTH achieved significant chart success, reaching #4 on the Beatport Electro House chart with Back 4 More. The same year, he released the Subterranean Grooves EP, a two-track project highlighting his deeper, groove-driven production style. Shortly after, he secured #1 on the Beatport Electro chart with Giving You Up, marking a major milestone in his rapidly growing career.
